data.narrative:
Agnikul Cosmos is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage, including Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift.

Two methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $40M total raised proxy, implying a $76M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria), yielding a median of $400M. A public-comp P/S anchoring approach cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 59.8x revenue across six public tickers, Agnikul Cosmos does not publicly disclose revenue, preventing meaningful multiplication.

The triangulated range spans $65M (floor, based on conservative stage progression) to $460M (ceiling, near peer-median), with a median implied value of $238M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median estimates reflects fundamental uncertainty around whether Agnikul Cosmos should trade toward early-stage financials or toward the more capital-intensive, proven-technology valuations of current peers. Revenue traction and development stage milestones will be critical to narrowing this range.
